name: Guru Enterprise Knowledge Management API
what_it_does: Guru enterprise knowledge management REST API for sales, support, and customer success teams to create, verify, and surface accurate knowledge cards with AI-powered search, Slack/browser integration, and expert verification workflow. Enables AI agents to manage knowledge card creation and expert assignment for knowledge base automation, handle card verification workflow and expiration tracking for knowledge freshness automation, access AI-powered search and question answering for knowledge retrieval automation, retrieve collection and board organization for knowledge taxonomy automation, manage browser extension and Slack bot knowledge surfacing for workflow integration automation, handle card analytics and usage tracking for knowledge effectiveness automation, access custom knowledge board creation for team-specific knowledge hub automation, retrieve card comment and feedback for knowledge improvement automation, manage bulk card import and migration for knowledge consolidation automation, and integrate Guru with Salesforce, Zendesk, Slack, Confluence, and HRIS for end-to-end knowledge management workflow.
use_cases: Creating verified knowledge cards from content management agents
Surfacing accurate answers from AI knowledge retrieval agents
Tracking knowledge freshness from verification workflow agents
Syncing knowledge from platform integration agents
not_for: General documentation and developer API docs (use Confluence or GitBook)
Customer-facing help center and support portal (use Zendesk Guide or Intercom Articles)
best_when: A sales, support, or customer success team wanting AI agents to automate knowledge card management, expert verification workflows, AI-powered knowledge surfacing, and team knowledge base maintenance through Guru's enterprise knowledge management platform.
avoid_when: CARD VERIFICATION WORKFLOW REQUIRES HUMAN EXPERT: Guru's core differentiation is expert-verified knowledge cards; automated card creation without human expert review defeats Guru's verification model; automated AI-generated card creation must route through human expert verification before marking cards as trusted; automated publishing of AI-generated cards without expert review creates unverified knowledge risk. KNOWLEDGE CARD SCOPE vs LONG-FORM DOCUMENTATION: Guru is optimized for bite-sized knowledge cards, not long-form documentation; automated content migration that imports long-form documentation as single cards creates unwieldy card format that doesn't align with Guru's search and surfacing model; implement card chunking for long-form content migration. BROWSER EXTENSION DEPENDENCY FOR CONTEXTUAL SURFACING: Guru's contextual knowledge surfacing relies on browser extension for automatic triggering; automated knowledge surfacing to agents without browser context must use explicit API search rather than trigger-based surfacing; API-based knowledge retrieval requires explicit query rather than contextual page detection.

auth.notes: Guru uses API key and OAuth 2.0 for integrations. REST API with JSON. Philadelphia, Pennsylvania HQ. Founded 2015 by Rick Nucci and Mitch Stewart. Backed by Thrive Capital, Slack Fund, Salesforce Ventures ($55M+ raised). Products: Knowledge cards, verification workflow, AI search, browser extension, Slack bot, Collections/Boards taxonomy. Integrations: Salesforce, Zendesk, Slack, Confluence, Workday, BambooHR. GDPR. SOC2. Serves mid-market to enterprise customer-facing teams. Competes with Notion, Confluence, and Slite for enterprise knowledge management.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free up to 3 users; limited cards and analytics
pricing.paid_tiers: Starter ($5/user/mo), Builder ($10/user/mo), Expert ($20/user/mo); annual contract discount
pricing.notes: Philadelphia PA. Thrive Capital/Slack Fund backed. Per-user subscription. Free tier (3 users). Annual discount.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits per API key; search and card queries throttled; webhooks for card create, update, verify, and archive events

known_agent_gotchas: COLLECTION vs BOARD vs CARD HIERARCHY: Guru organizes knowledge in Collections → Boards → Cards; automated card creation must specify correct board ID within correct collection; automated content organization without hierarchy awareness creates cards in wrong collection scope, limiting visibility to wrong team audience

CARD VERIFICATION STATUS IMPACT ON SEARCH RANKING: Guru search surfaces verified cards above unverified; automated card creation that skips verification workflow creates cards that rank lower in search results; automated knowledge importation without triggering expert verification creates knowledge base with low-confidence content surfacing

CARD EXPIRATION WORKFLOW FOR AUTOMATED FRESHNESS: Guru cards can be set with expiration intervals requiring re-verification; automated knowledge workflows must handle card expiration events via webhook and route to expert verifier; automated systems that ignore expiration webhooks create knowledge base with expired (stale) cards that users still surface in search

SEARCH QUERY CONTEXT FOR AI-POWERED RETRIEVAL: Guru's AI search provides best results with conversational or contextual queries; automated knowledge retrieval using keyword-only queries may miss semantically relevant cards; automated question answering via Guru API should use natural language query format rather than keyword list for optimal AI retrieval accuracy

BULK CARD IMPORT FORMAT REQUIREMENTS: Guru bulk import supports specific card format (rich text, specific heading structure); automated content migration from other platforms must transform source content to Guru card format; automated migration of Confluence/Notion content to Guru without format transformation creates poorly structured cards that don't align with Guru card browsing UX
agent_readiness.security_notes: Enterprise knowledge management. SOC2, GDPR. API key/OAuth2. US. Knowledge card, team, and search data.

security_score_components.security_notes: Knowledge management. SOC2, GDPR. API key/OAuth2. US. Team knowledge and search data.
